title: Thyroid function result review

investigations.text: Supports clinician suspicion context, mood or anxiety context, TSH, FT4, FT3, biotin history, repeat testing when symptoms change, and TPO antibody testing in recommendation-qualified cases.
investigations.rationale: The exact thyroid-testing section supports result source and date, symptom and negative context, biotin or acute-illness considerations, focused endocrine assessment and clinician-reviewed thyroid results without independently interpreting them.
investigations.evidence_extract: Supports clinician suspicion context, mood or anxiety context, TSH, FT4, FT3, biotin history, repeat testing when symptoms change, and TPO antibody testing in recommendation-qualified cases.

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.2.1–1.3.2 — suspected dysfunction testing and antibodies

medication.text: Supports shared decision documentation, thyroid-medicine interaction information, and specific thyroid-enlargement warning symptoms including shortness of breath, rapid growth, hoarse voice, and swallowing difficulty.
medication.rationale: The exact information section supports patient concerns, previous endocrine correspondence, clinician plan, safety net, follow-up and questions only when discussed.
medication.evidence_extract: Supports shared decision documentation, thyroid-medicine interaction information, and specific thyroid-enlargement warning symptoms including shortness of breath, rapid growth, hoarse voice, and swallowing difficulty.

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.1.1–1.1.6 — information and thyroid-enlargement warning symptoms

additions.text: Supports clinician suspicion context, mood or anxiety context, TSH, FT4, FT3, biotin history, repeat testing when symptoms change, and TPO antibody testing in recommendation-qualified cases.

source_limitations: TSH, FT4, FT3 values and ranges, symptoms, pregnancy, acute illness, biotin, medicines, pituitary context, diagnosis, treatment and timing remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
